title: Telesat eyes defense role with laser comms test ahead of LEO debut
summary: Telesat is expanding into the defense sector by testing laser communications in preparation for its upcoming low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite deployment. The company plans a 2027 demonstration for NASA while the Pentagon seeks faster space data transport solutions.

title: Sift raises $42 million in Series B round
summary: Sift, a Southern California startup, raised $42 million in a Series B funding round to expand its engineering team and develop tools for analyzing hardware sensor data. The funding will support infrastructure for AI-controlled devices, including modern rockets and satellites.

title: Infinity Avionics Unveils Aquila: The Next-Generation Space Imaging Solution for Complex Orbital Operations
summary: Infinity Avionics introduced Aquila, a next-generation space imaging solution for complex orbital operations, after its debut at IAC Sydney. The commercial availability of Aquila marks a significant advancement in smart vision systems for the NewSpace economy.

title: Airbase raises $5 million to tackle spectrum bottleneck as FCC eyes new space uses
summary: Airbase, a New York-based software startup, raised $5 million to modernize government coordination of radio frequencies for satellites and 5G networks. The funding aims to address spectrum bottlenecks as the FCC explores new space applications.

title: Musk wants to go to the moon. But how will he build his ‘self-growing city’?
summary: Elon Musk has announced plans to establish a self-sustaining city on the Moon, though his ambitious claims are often met with skepticism. The feasibility of such a 'self-growing city' remains uncertain.

title: Pave Space raises $40 million to develop European heavy kickstage
summary: Swiss startup Pave Space secured $40 million in funding to develop a heavy kickstage orbital transfer vehicle, enabling rapid satellite deployment from LEO to final orbits in hours rather than months.

title: NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations
summary: NASA is revising its approach to fostering commercial space stations to replace the ISS, citing slow progress in market development. The new strategy aims to accelerate the transition to private orbital outposts.

title: Geopolitical shifts bring opportunities and headaches for satellite execs
summary: Geopolitical shifts are creating significant business opportunities for satellite communications while also introducing supply chain and regulatory challenges, according to industry executives at the Satellite Conference in Washington, D.C.

title: Moog taps Redwire to provide solar arrays for Meteor
summary: Redwire secured a $12.8 million contract from Moog to provide solar arrays for Moog's Meteor satellite bus. The Extensible Low-Profile Solar Array (ELSA) wings will power the satellite for an undisclosed national security customer.

title: OrbitsIQ Global Announces Breakthrough in Space-Based IoT Connectivity
summary: OrbitsIQ Global announced a major breakthrough in space-based IoT connectivity, achieved through a collaborative effort. The innovation leverages AI-driven secure connectivity across terrestrial and satellite networks.

title: Rocket shines under the northern lights | Space photo of the day for March 25, 2026
summary: A stunning photo captures the northern lights illuminating the night sky above Isar Aerospace's Spectrum rocket. The image was featured as the Space photo of the day for March 25, 2026.

title: Like 'accelerating from stationary to supersonic flight': Europe's Hera probe boosts speed, stays on course for November asteroid rendezvous
summary: Europe's Hera probe has successfully completed a major deep-space maneuver, remaining on course for its planned November rendezvous with the asteroid system targeted by NASA's DART mission in 2022.

title: Europe plans EPIC SpaceX mission to send 4 astronauts to the ISS before its retirement
summary: The European Space Agency is planning a dedicated SpaceX Crew Dragon mission, named EPIC, to send four astronauts to the ISS before its retirement. This mission aims to enhance Europe's astronaut time and experience in space.

title: Scientists finally solve century-old mystery of star with unexpected X-ray emissions
summary: Astronomers used the XRISM X-ray spacecraft to solve a century-old mystery involving a star with unexpected X-ray emissions, revealing it is being devoured by a stellar companion.

title: 1 week away! NASA gearing up to launch Artemis 2 astronauts around the moon on April 1
summary: NASA is preparing to launch the Artemis 2 mission on April 1, marking the first crewed moon mission since the Apollo era. The launch is just one week away.

title: Finished Starfleet Academy? Boldly binge-watch the superb Star Trek: Strange New Worlds, just $2.99 a month thanks to this Paramount+ deal
summary: Paramount+ is offering a discount deal for streaming 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds,' making it available for $2.99 a month. Season 4 of the show is upcoming, appealing to fans of the original Star Trek series.

title: Scientists discover mirror of our solar system in 2 exoplanets forming around a star
summary: Astronomers discovered two exoplanets forming around a distant infant star, providing insights into the early solar system. This finding acts as a time capsule for studying planetary formation billions of years ago.

title: Amazon Spring Sale deals for stargazing 2026: Huge savings on telescopes, binoculars and cameras
summary: Amazon is offering discounts on telescopes, binoculars, and cameras in its Spring Sale 2026, targeting stargazing enthusiasts. The sale includes significant savings on skywatching optics and photography equipment.

title: Rocket Lab launch of Europe's 1st 2 'Celeste' navigation satellites scrubbed due to weather
summary: Rocket Lab's planned launch of Europe's first two 'Celeste' navigation satellites was scrubbed due to weather conditions. The mission was scheduled for early Wednesday morning (March 25).

title: NASA's lunar Gateway space station is out. Moon bases are in
summary: NASA has decided to remove the lunar Gateway space station from its Artemis mission plans, shifting focus to establishing bases on the lunar surface instead.

title: Kona Storms Flood Oʻahu
summary: Back-to-back subtropical cyclones in March caused severe flash flooding on several Hawaiian Islands, including Oʻahu. The Kona storms resulted in significant damage and disruption to the region.

title: NASA PC-12 Aircraft Makes Move to Support Flight Research Across Agency
summary: NASA's Pilatus PC-12 aircraft has been relocated to Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, to support flight research across the agency. The aircraft was originally acquired in 2022 by NASA's Glenn Research Center for advanced technology development.

title: Reminders of Where We’ve Been, Where We’re Going
summary: NASA announced transformative initiatives on March 24, 2026, during an event showcasing Moon rocks. The initiatives aim to advance American leadership in space and include plans to send astronauts to the lunar surface for the first time in over 50 years.

title: 3 Ways Students Can Get Involved With Artemis
summary: NASA's Artemis program invites students to participate in establishing a sustainable lunar presence and advancing spaceflight technologies for Mars. The initiative aims to engage students in shaping the future of space exploration.

title: NASA Selects Finalists in Student Aircraft Maintenance Competition
summary: NASA selected seven student teams as finalists in the 2026 Gateways to Blue Skies Competition, focusing on addressing aircraft maintenance challenges in the U.S. aviation industry.

title: NASA X-Ray Mission Gets Fresh Look at 2,000-Year-Old Supernova
summary: NASA's IXPE mission has observed the 2,000-year-old supernova RCW 86, enhancing previous findings from the Chandra X-ray Observatory by revealing a large cavity around the system that influenced its expansion.

title: La NASA presenta iniciativas para cumplir con la política espacial nacional de Estados Unidos
summary: NASA unveiled transformative initiatives during its 'Ignition' event to align with the U.S. National Space Policy under President Trump and bolster American leadership in space. The agency's actions aim to advance its mission in line with national priorities.

title: Copernicus Trajectory Design and Optimization System
summary: Copernicus is a generalized spacecraft trajectory design and optimization system developed at the University of Texas at Austin, capable of solving complex trajectory problems for various space missions.

title: NASA Research Proposes Technology to Seek Earth-Like Exoplanets
summary: NASA is developing technologies to detect Earth-like exoplanets by analyzing reflected light, which could reveal signs of water and oxygen. This research aims to advance our understanding of potentially habitable planets beyond our solar system.

title: NASA’s Water-Hunting Tool Will Help Scout Moon’s South Pole
summary: NASA is contributing a water-detecting instrument, the Neutron Spectrometer System (NSS), to the Lunar Polar Exploration (LUPEX) mission led by JAXA and ISRO to scout for ice on the Moon's south pole. This collaboration supports future human exploration efforts.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for 2028, during a presentation led by Administrator Jared Isaacman and other agency leaders.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to its Starbase launch pads in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starships, aiming to increase launch cadence. The activity highlights SpaceX's focus on enhancing infrastructure for more frequent and efficient space missions.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the ISS from a repaired launch pad at Baikonur Cosmodrome. This launch occurred nearly four months after the Soyuz MS-28 mission from the same site.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has revealed plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the crewed lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. The development brings the next Starship launch one step closer to reality.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine expected missions, including launches by SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Isar Aerospace's Spectrum.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin has introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense mission concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The concept combines multiple technologies to detect and deflect potential threats.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for lunar and Martian missions at its Starfactory facility, transitioning from steel production to Starship development. The company aims to expand its manufacturing capabilities to support ambitious space exploration goals.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the context of space industry news, but no specific details or context are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly caused a loss of connection.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), aiming to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator (LSS) to verify its functionality before a planned 2027 launch. The spacecraft's 26 ultra-sensitive cameras are being exposed to extreme temperatures and vacuum conditions to simulate deep space.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 software on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky traditional antennas.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic and mobility systems. This initiative aims to advance space technology research and education.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space has secured $40 million in funding to advance its in-space propulsion technology, which uses storable bipropellant to simplify and reduce the cost of launch operations compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase, a new startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation for space missions. The company aims to address the growing demand for spectrum management as orbital missions increase.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has revised priorities to foster a commercially driven lunar economy with surface infrastructure. The updated plan aims to reshape NASA's strategic direction.

title: One-On-One With SDA’s GP Sandhoo
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) has paused operations on its first operational satellite tranche due to minor issues, delaying benefits for troops. SDA Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo discussed the 'strategic pause' in a recent interview.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio Hosted Payload Platform
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio Hosted Payload Platform, a new model where satellites remain in orbit while payloads are added or removed as needed. This innovation aims to provide flexible and cost-effective solutions for payload hosting in space.

title: Ramon.Space, Ingrasys Aim To Fly Prototype Orbital Data Center In 2027
summary: Ramon.Space and Ingrasys plan to launch a prototype orbital data center by 2027, joining the growing competition in space-based data processing. The initiative aims to leverage space environments for advanced computing applications.

title: Beyond the Rocket: The Digital Infrastructure of the Artemis II Mission
summary: The Artemis II mission's digital infrastructure is crucial but often overlooked, differing significantly from Apollo-era technology. NASA's digital architecture enables the mission's advanced capabilities.

title: Contrivian Launches New Product Bundling Amazon Leo & Starlink
summary: Contrivian has launched a new product that bundles satellite connectivity services from Amazon's Project Kuiper (Leo) and SpaceX's Starlink. This offering allows customers to use both networks without having to choose between providers.

title: SpaceX v. Amazon Heats Up at the FCC
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are engaged in a regulatory dispute at the FCC regarding their satellite constellations. The public dueling highlights competition between the two companies in the satellite communications sector.

title: High Fire Volume Systems: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The Pentagon's Golden Dome program faces challenges with traditional space-based interceptors, leading to exploration of alternative approaches like deep magazine systems and directed energy. These alternatives aim to address engineering, procurement, and scalability issues.

title: Geopolitical Shakeups and Capital Wars Push Top Operators to Iterate Faster
summary: Geopolitical conflicts and rising concerns are highlighting the critical role of satellite communications, prompting top operators to accelerate innovation. Commercial satellite leaders emphasize the need for faster iteration in response to global tensions.

title: Launchers Cite Busy Manifests and Scarcity as Commercial Demand Grows
summary: Launch providers report extremely busy manifests for the next two years due to growing commercial demand. SpaceX is highlighted as a key player in the heavy-lift launch competition.

title: Lynk and SES CEOs Talk D2D Demand, As D2D Merger Awaits Completion
summary: The CEOs of Lynk and SES discuss growing demand for direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity amid market competition and geopolitical challenges. The merger of D2D services is pending completion despite these obstacles.

title: SDA Takes ‘Strategic Pause’ on Launches to Work Through Fixes
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) has paused launches to address and implement fixes. This strategic pause aims to improve satellite production and acquisition processes.

title: Astranis and Oman Group Execs Detail How Middle East Conflict is Driving Demand For Sovereign Satellite Services
summary: The ongoing Middle East conflict is increasing demand for sovereign satellite services, as highlighted by Astranis and MB Group executives during SATShow Week. The partnership between Astranis and MB Group aims to address this growing need.

title: Redwire, Moog Sign Deal to Integrate Redwire ELSA Solar Array Wings with Moog Meteor Bus
summary: Redwire and Moog have signed a $12.8 million contract to integrate Redwire's ELSA solar array wings with Moog's largest satellite bus during SATShow Week. This partnership aims to enhance satellite power capabilities.

title: Interview With Mini-Circuits CEO Jin Bains at SATShow Week
summary: The demand for radio frequency (RF) components is rising due to the surge in satellites in orbit. Mini-Circuits, led by CEO Jin Bains, is highlighted for its role in supporting satellite systems.

title: Amazon Leo Readies 200+ Satellites for Orbit as It Ramps Up Launch Schedule
summary: Amazon's Leo project has prepared over 200 satellites for launch, loaded onto six dispensers at Cape Canaveral. The company is accelerating its launch schedule to deploy the satellites into orbit in the coming months.

title: Space Force, Industry Leaders Warn of Critical Gaps in Orbital Intelligence
summary: The U.S. Space Force and industry leaders highlight significant gaps in orbital intelligence despite advanced tracking systems. These gaps pose risks to space domain awareness and national security.

title: Interview With TE Connectivity Principal Engineer Bruce Conway at SATShow Week
summary: TE Connectivity's Principal Engineer Bruce Conway discusses the company's interconnect solutions for satellites and solar panels, highlighting its deep space heritage during SATShow Week.
